description: Scroll the LinkedIn feed, collect top posts by engagement (likes + comments + shares), return structured list sorted by total engagement descending.

reasoning.research: LinkedIn feed renders as a dynamic list of article elements. Each post (feed item) is wrapped in a container with class .feed-shared-update-v2 or exposed as role=article. Engagement counts are in .social-details-social-counts__reactions-count for likes and .social-details-social-counts__comments for comments. Author info lives in .update-components-actor__title.
reasoning.selector_strategy: Prefer role-based selectors for stability. Fall back to class selectors for engagement counts since LinkedIn does not expose those as ARIA roles. Use document.querySelectorAll for bulk extraction rather than individual waits.
reasoning.anti_bot_notes: Scroll with random human-like delays (800-1500ms between scrolls). Extract in a single pass after scrolling completes to minimize DOM queries. Do not click any engagement buttons during discovery.
reasoning.llm_learnings: LinkedIn paginates feed lazily. 3-5 scrolls yield ~15-25 posts. Filter threshold of 50 total engagement removes low-signal noise. Posts older than 7 days rarely appear in default feed sort.
reasoning.performance: Expected run time: 8-15 seconds (navigation 3s + 4 scrolls x 1.2s + extraction 1s). No fill_slowly needed for read-only recipe.

feed_posts.note: Primary feed post container. Falls back to role=article if class changes.

feed_posts_fallback.note: ARIA fallback — broader but stable across redesigns.

author_name.note: Author display name within each post container.

post_text.note: Post body text. Truncate to 200 chars for snippet.

likes_count.note: Reaction/like count. Parse integer from text (may contain 'K').

post_url.note: Canonical post URL. Alternative: look for urn:li: in page links.

execution_trace.note: Must be logged in. Uses persistent session.
Human-like scroll to trigger lazy loading of feed posts.
Bulk extraction via page.evaluate(). Parse engagement counts as integers.
